challenges.title: Hex and RGB Colors
challenges.description: Write two CSS rules: (1) target `h1` elements and set `color` using a hex value (e.g. `#ff0000`), (2) target `p` elements and set `background-color` using `rgb()` notation (e.g. `rgb(0, 128, 255)`). Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'h1' in css.lower(), 'Should target h1 elements'
assert 'p' in css.lower(), 'Should target p elements'
assert '#' in css, 'Should use a hex color value'
assert 'rgb(' in css.lower(), 'Should use rgb() notation'
assert 'color' in css.lower(), 'Should set the color property'
assert 'background-color' in css.lower(), 'Should set the background-color property'
hints: Hex colors start with # followed by 6 hex digits: #rrggbb
RGB notation uses `rgb(red, green, blue)` where each value is 0–255.

challenges.title: RGBA, HSL, and Named Colors
challenges.description: Write three CSS rules: (1) target `.overlay` and set `background-color` using `rgba()` with an alpha of `0.5`, (2) target `.accent` and set `color` using `hsl()` notation, (3) target `body` and set `background-color` to a named color like `cornflowerblue`. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert '.overlay' in css, 'Should target .overlay'
assert '.accent' in css, 'Should target .accent'
assert 'body' in css.lower(), 'Should target body'
assert 'rgba(' in css.lower(), 'Should use rgba() notation'
assert '0.5' in css or '.5' in css, 'Should include an alpha value of 0.5'
assert 'hsl(' in css.lower(), 'Should use hsl() notation'
assert 'background-color' in css.lower(), 'Should use background-color property'
assert 'color' in css.lower(), 'Should use color property'
hints: RGBA adds an alpha channel: `rgba(255, 0, 0, 0.5)` for 50% transparent red.
